title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Execution Trace: Quarantine Publish State
summary: An upload handler trusts client filenames and content types, accepts unlimited bodies, publishes bytes before scanning, and leaves partial state when the caller disconnects. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quarantine publish state increment in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Model admitted, uploading, quarantined, scanning, accepted, rejected, published, and deleted transitions with authorized owners.
Combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Quarantine Publish State · Theory Quarantine Publish State
steps.instruction:
